scenario.title: Claim for malfunction after unauthorized third-party repair
scenario.description: Customer submits a claim for hardware failure that occurred shortly after a third-party repair where the device was opened and non-Apple parts were installed.

service_fee.notes: Service fee does not apply as there is no accidental damage claim; however, eligibility for free repair or replacement is uncertain.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to service (including upgrades and expansions) if such was performed by anyone who is not a representative of Apple or an Apple Authorized Service Provider.

The Plan does not apply to an iPhone with a serial number that has been altered, defaced or removed, or has been modified to alter its functionality or capability without the written permission of the manufacturer.
Apple will repair or exchange the Covered iPhone if a defect in materials and workmanship arises during the Plan term.

scenario.reasoning: The failure closely follows an unauthorized repair involving opening the device and installing non-Apple parts, which the Plan explicitly excludes under Sections 4.2(ii)(d) and 4.2(iii). However, the timing suggests a potential manufacturing defect triggering the failure, as per Section 3.1. This tension creates ambiguity whether failure is due to unauthorized modification or an inherent defect, necessitating investigation of serial integrity, repair history, and detailed claim information to resolve coverage eligibility. No service fee applies as this is not an ADH claim, and since the claim may be denied, the counts toward ADH limit is 'no'.

persona.education: Some college, but less than 1 year
persona.household type: Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18
persona.mannerisms: Often smiles and maintains eye contact when speaking
persona.personal time: Spends free time in her garden or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer submits a claim for hardware failure that occurred shortly after a third-party repair where the device was opened and non-Apple parts were installed.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It started having some hardware issues recently, shortly after I had it repaired by a third-party service. They opened the device and installed some non-Apple parts. I'm hoping you can assist me with this.
messages.raw_text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 Pro Max. It started having some hardware issues recently, shortly after I had it repaired by a third-party service. They opened the device and installed some non-Apple parts. I'm hoping you can assist me with this.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's Serial Number so we can begin verifying your coverage?
messages.raw_text: Could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's Serial Number so we can begin verifying your coverage?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-5523987461 and the serial number for my iPhone is F4L9J7K1N8P2. Please let me know what you need next.
messages.raw_text: Sure, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-5523987461 and the serial number for my iPhone is F4L9J7K1N8P2. Please let me know what you need next.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. Please describe the symptoms or issues you are experiencing with your iPhone 14 Pro Max.
messages.raw_text: Thank you. Please describe the symptoms or issues you are experiencing with your iPhone 14 Pro Max.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly. The phone has been randomly shutting down and the screen sometimes goes unresponsive. It also doesn’t charge properly at times, even when I use the original charger. It’s been pretty frustrating, especially since it just started after the repair. I really hope we can get this sorted out quickly.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. The phone has been randomly shutting down and the screen sometimes goes unresponsive. It also doesn’t charge properly at times, even when I use the original charger. It’s been pretty frustrating, especially since it just started after the repair. I really hope we can get this sorted out quickly.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the details. To better understand the problem, could you tell me your iPhone model again and the current iOS version it's running? Also, have you noticed any error messages, or have you tried any troubleshooting steps so far?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the details. To better understand the problem, could you tell me your iPhone model again and the current iOS version it's running? Also, have you noticed any error messages, or have you tried any troubleshooting steps so far?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I have the iPhone 14 Pro Max, 512 GB variant. The iOS version is 17.0. I haven’t seen any specific error messages, but I did try restarting the phone and updating the iOS just to rule out software issues. The problems still persist despite those steps.
messages.raw_text: I have the iPhone 14 Pro Max, 512 GB variant. The iOS version is 17.0. I haven’t seen any specific error messages, but I did try restarting the phone and updating the iOS just to rule out software issues. The problems still persist despite those steps.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I appreciate the information. Since the issue started after a third-party repair involving non-Apple parts, are you aware of any physical damage like drops or liquid exposure that might have contributed to these issues?
messages.raw_text: I appreciate the information. Since the issue started after a third-party repair involving non-Apple parts, are you aware of any physical damage like drops or liquid exposure that might have contributed to these issues?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I’m quite careful with my phone and have not dropped it or exposed it to any liquids. The problems really began only after the third-party repair, so I believe that’s directly related.
messages.raw_text: No, I’m quite careful with my phone and have not dropped it or exposed it to any liquids. The problems really began only after the third-party repair, so I believe that’s directly related.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for clarifying that. I have gathered all the necessary information to begin processing your claim. Is there anything else you would like to add or any other assistance you need before I proceed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for clarifying that. I have gathered all the necessary information to begin processing your claim. Is there anything else you would like to add or any other assistance you need before I proceed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that covers everything for now. Thank you for your help. Please go ahead with processing the claim. I appreciate your prompt assistance.
messages.raw_text: No, that covers everything for now. Thank you for your help. Please go ahead with processing the claim. I appreciate your prompt assistance.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will be processed shortly, and you will be notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: Your claim will be processed shortly, and you will be notified as soon as it is completed. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day! <END>
